changes_made: Renamed k8s_admission_controller_vulnerability_scan_on_admission to k8s_admission_vulnerability_scan_on_admission for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's requirement for vulnerability scanning. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but a minor adjustment was made for consistency. The functions map well to Kubernetes security practices, specifically focusing on image vulnerability scanning and admission control.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of 'admission' as the resource name for admission controllers
title: 3_11_2: 3.11.2 Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems and applications are identified
step1_reasoning: The control requires periodic vulnerability scanning of systems and applications. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that container images are scanned for vulnerabilities before being deployed. This can be achieved by integrating image scanning tools that check for vulnerabilities at the time of image admission. Additionally, setting the image pull policy to 'Always' ensures that the latest version of the image is used, which should have been scanned for vulnerabilities.
